steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence pairs two examples with "and": "the word 'lasso' comes from the Spanish word lazo, and 'stockade' (a fence) __________ from estacada." The blank must match the verb tense of the first clause to keep the sentence parallel.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — will be coming: future progressive tense. The first clause uses simple present "comes," so this shifts tense and breaks parallelism. ✗
  • Choice B — will come: simple future tense. Again mismatches "comes" in the first clause; no reason is given to switch to future. ✗
  • Choice C — comes: simple present tense, identical to "comes" in the first clause. Both clauses now state a timeless fact in the same tense. ✓
  • Choice D — had come: past perfect tense. This implies the action was completed before some other past event, which contradicts the parallel structure and adds unneeded complexity. ✗
That is the correct answer.
